Agrotourism Discovery

Ibirunga Coffee: From Crop to Cup

Journey through the mist-covered foothills of Kinigi. Discover the meticulous craft behind Rwanda's world-class specialty coffee while supporting local farming cooperatives.

Book the Experiencearrow_forward

A Legacy of Soil and Community

At the heart of Musanze, the volcanic soil of the Ibirunga region provides a unique mineral richness that breathes life into every Arabica bean. Our coffee tour isn't just a walk — it's an immersive narrative of resilience and heritage.

Working hand-in-hand with the Kinigi Farmers Cooperative, we offer travelers a rare glimpse into the life of the people who have mastered the art of high-altitude cultivation. Every sip you take supports sustainable agriculture and empowers local families through fair-trade practices.

2,500mElevation
100%Red Bourbon
88+Cupping Score

What You Will Learn

Master the critical stages that transform a humble cherry into liquid gold.

history_edu
Step 01

History

Discover how coffee first arrived in Rwanda in 1904 and its evolution into a premium national treasure.

terrain
Step 02

Regions & Soil

Learn why volcanic nitrogen-rich soil and high altitude are the secrets to the unique acidity profile.

potted_plant
Step 03

Varieties

Specializing in the rare Arabica Red Bourbon variety, known for sweet, citrusy floral notes.

compost
Step 04

Fertilizing

Organic techniques using recycled coffee pulp and local minerals to maintain ecological balance.

health_and_safety
Step 05

Disease Control

Natural, sustainable pest management strategies used by the cooperative to protect every harvest.

pan_tool
Step 06

Harvesting

Hand-picking only the ripest "cherries" with surgical precision to ensure maximum quality.

factory
Step 07

Processing

Washing, pulping, and fermenting in mountain-spring water before sun-drying on raised beds.

local_cafe
Step 08

Roasting & Tasting

The finale: roasting on-site and a professional cupping session with the cooperative baristas.

Farm-to-Cup Journey

Follow the 10-step journey from the volcanic soil to your cup — a hands-on, start-to-finish experience.

STEP 01

Farm & Soil

The volcanic Virunga soil and high-altitude growing conditions create the perfect environment for specialty Arabica coffee.

Awaken Your Senses

There is no substitute for the aroma of freshly roasted Kinigi coffee in the crisp mountain air. Our tours provide a full sensory education — smell the jasmine notes, see the vibrant cherry hues, and touch the coarse volcanic soil.

psychology

Educational

eco

Eco-Friendly

groups

Community Driven

workspace_premium

Gourmet Quality

Photo & Video Gallery

Moments captured during our tours — from the plantation to the cup.

Tell Us About Your Visit

Ready to experience the heights of Rwanda? Book your private or group coffee tour today. Duration: 4-6 hours. Includes transportation from Musanze and a fresh bag of Ibirunga Coffee.

  • check_circleExpert Local Guide
  • check_circleRoasting & Tasting Workshop
  • check_circleTraditional Rwandan Lunch Included

The number entered here will allow you to book for people in your group. e.g. if you are 3 people, enter 3 and you'll be able to book for 3 people only.

Total Cost$150

Rate: $150/visitor × 1 visitor

Pioneering sustainable and transformative travel experiences across the heart of Rwanda. Member of the Rwanda Tour Operators Association.
